title: molinia caerulea subsp. varia
intro: Molinia caerulea subsp. varia, also known as purple moor-grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae and is native to Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of purple moor-grass include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as supporting the immune system and promoting wound healing. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
